scope: Feature engineering techniques that survive and are essential inside deep learning pipelines.
key_concepts: Deep learning models cannot consume raw, real-world objects directly; they require structured numerical inputs, which demands explicit feature engineering.
Tokenization, specifically subword tokenization like BPE, is a critical feature engineering choice that dictates how text is fragmented before the network processes it.
Data augmentation engineers deliberate variations into the input data, forcing the neural network to learn robust, invariant representations.
Classic numeric preprocessing, such as scaling and normalization, remains absolutely essential for the stable convergence of deep neural networks.
These techniques demonstrate that human-designed transformations have not disappeared; they now live at the very front of the deep learning pipeline.

definitions.definition: Proses memecah teks mentah menjadi unit-unit diskret (seperti kata atau subword) agar dapat diproses oleh model.
Teknik memperluas dataset secara artifisial dengan menerapkan modifikasi yang tidak mengubah label target asli.

examples: Using Byte-Pair Encoding to split an unknown complex word into recognizable subwords.
Applying random horizontal flips and color jittering to training images to prevent a vision model from memorizing specific orientations.

relations.precedes: ch15_s07 (how tabular data specifically is adapted for deep learning).
relations.follows: ch15_s05 (which established that FE merely shifted form in the DL era).

exclusions: Do not detail the step-by-step algorithms of specific tokenizers like WordPiece or BPE.
